content.problemStatement:
You are given a standard 8×88 \times 88×8 chessboard which has exactly 222 rooks on it and no other pieces. The rows are numbered 111 to 888 from bottom to top, and the columns are numbered 111 to 888 from left to right. The cell at the intersection of the iii-th column and jjj-th row is denoted (i,j)(i,j)(i,j).

Given the initial positions of the rooks in the form of coordinates (X1,Y1)(X_1,Y_1)(X1​,Y1​) and (X2,Y2)(X_2,Y_2)(X2​,Y2​), you need to tell whether the 222 rooks currently attack each other or not. Assume, each square can contain at most one piece.

Rooks can only travel in straight lines along the row or column they are placed at, and can't jump over other pieces. For a more detailed explanation of the moves of rooks, along with images, please click here.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contain four space-separated integers each X1,Y1,X2,Y2X_1, Y_1, X_2, Y_2X1​,Y1​,X2​,Y2​ - (X1,Y1)(X_1,Y_1)(X1​,Y1​) is the position of the first rook and (X2,Y2)(X_2,Y_2)(X2​,Y2​) is the position of the second rook.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line `YES` (without quotes) if the rooks attack each other, and `NO` otherwise.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `YeS`, `YEs`, `yes` and `yeS` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains four space‑separated integers X1 Y1 X2 Y2 denoting the coordinates of the two rooks. 1 ≤ X1, X2, Y1, Y2 ≤ 8 and (X1,Y1) ≠ (X2,Y2).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the two rooks can attack each other (i.e., they share the same row or the same column) and "NO" otherwise.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: Same column (column 2) → YES.\nTest case 2: Same row (row 1) → YES.\nTest case 3: Different rows and columns → NO.

hidden.label: general case no attack different row and column

solution.approach: Two rooks attack each other if and only if they are placed on the same row (Y1 == Y2) or the same column (X1 == X2). For each test case we just compare the coordinates and output the result. The algorithm runs in O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
